title: Explainer: missing data, attrition & intention-to-treat basics
context: Missing data and attrition are common in oncology trials, and the handling of missing observations materially affects the validity of reported results. Clinicians need to understand the difference between intention-to-treat (ITT), per-protocol (PP), and modified ITT analyses, and how missing data assumptions (MCAR, MAR, MNAR) affect the interpretation of results. This is a backlog extension of the core set, warranting biostatistician review, and builds on the core survival explainers.
objective: Author, biostatistician-sign-off, and publish an explainer on missing data, attrition, and intention-to-treat analysis — covering the ITT/PP/mITT distinction, the missing data assumptions, and common traps in how missing-data handling is reported and interpreted — with a reproducible worked example on open/synthetic data.
acceptanceCriteria: The explainer provides plain and precise definitions of ITT, per-protocol, and modified ITT analyses and when each is appropriate; an explicit commonTrap section covers misinterpreting a per-protocol result as a primary efficacy estimate without acknowledging the selection bias.

The three missing data mechanisms are explained: MCAR (missing completely at random), MAR (missing at random), MNAR (missing not at random); the implications of each assumption for result validity are stated.
Common traps in attrition reporting are covered: high dropout without sensitivity analysis; differential dropout between arms; MNAR data handled as if MAR.
A worked example on open/synthetic data has a manifest entry; all figures and numbers emitted by committed script and diff-matched by repro-005.
Every conceptual claim cites an authoritative methods source; howToReadInAPaper checklist included.
Structural 'educational, not clinical advice' framing present.
Biostatistician statsSignoff recorded; clinical-framing and accessibility reviews passed.
Zero stated statistical falsehoods; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: Reporting guidelines: CONSORT (missing data extension); ICH E9(R1) addendum on estimands; methods literature on missing data (cited per-claim)
output: A published, biostatistician-signed-off missing data, attrition, and ITT explainer with 100% reproducible worked example, full provenance, all claims cited, accessibility passed, and structural 'not clinical advice' frame.
